query: We're publishing a one-time release of average annual income by 5-digit ZIP code in the U.S. Should we apply differential privacy?
expected_behavior: Does NOT immediately apply DP to this release; explicitly considers whether DP is the right tool given the threat model (one-time release, typically thousands of households per cell, low re-identification risk for standard ZIP cells)

Identifies k-anonymity (with cell-suppression for ZIPs that fall below the k threshold) as the canonical and typically sufficient defense for one-time aggregate release on cells with large underlying populations — recommends this over DP for the default case

Names the specific conditions under which the recommendation flips to requiring DP — repeated / longitudinal releases that compose, very small ZIP cohorts (rural ZIPs with under 100 households), or an explicit regulator mandate — so the user understands when k-anonymity stops being enough rather than treating DP as a universal answer
